title: allium schoenoprasum subsp. alpinum
intro: Allium schoenoprasum subsp. alpinum is a subspecies of the wild chives plant, native to high mountainous regions and characterized by its compact growth and delicate flavor. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the severity of colds and flu. The health benefits of Allium schoenoprasum subsp. alpinum include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory health, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of this plant are often used in teas, infusions, and salves, and can also be consumed raw as a culinary herb.
